code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test08-OCT-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, mild ketonuria, and low specific gravity, with no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). The urine is cloudy and brown with an ammoniacal odor. These findings suggest possible renal dysfunction, such as glomerular disease or tubular injury, and further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
